questions.question: Which of the following was the first planet to have its motions plotted across the sky during the second millennium BC?
questions.explanation: Venus was the first planet to have its motions plotted across the sky, as early as the second millennium BC.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is incorrect about the Planet Mars?

options.label: A
options.text: It has two permanent polar ice caps

options.label: B
options.text: After the Moon, it is the brightest object in our night sky

options.label: C
options.text: Liquid water cannot exist on the surface of Mars due to low atmospheric pressure

options.label: D
options.text: Mars is less dense than Earth

answer.label: B
answer.text: After the Moon, it is the brightest object in our night sky

questions.explanation: Second statement is incorrect because Venus is the second brightest object in our night sky after Moon.

questions.question: Who among the following proposed Helio-centric model of solar system?
questions.explanation: Copernicus proposed Helio-centric model. According to this model, sun stands at the center of the universe, and Moon, the Earth, and remaining planets revolve around the sun.

questions.question: Geo centric model of solar system was proposed by:
questions.explanation: Geo-centric theory of solar system was proposed by Ptolemy 2000 years ago. This theory states that all celestial objects, stars, the sun and the planets, all revolved around the earth.

questions.question: Who among the following proposed Laws of planetary motion?
questions.explanation: Kepler proposed Laws of planetary motion. These three laws describes the behavior of planets in the heliocentric model; The three laws of Kepler can be stated as follows: 1. Law of orbits: All planets move in elliptical orbits with the Sun situated at one of the foci of the ellipse 2. Law of areas: The line that joins any planet to the sun sweeps equal areas in equal intervals of time. 3. Law of periods: The Square of the time period of revolution of a planet is proportional to the cube of the semi-major axis of the ellipse traced out by the planet. The law of areas can be understood as a consequence of conservation of angular momentum which is valid for any central force.
